title: Gingered Korean Romano Pepper Bibimbap
description: A flavorful and spicy Korean-inspired dish featuring the unique taste of Romano peppers, perfect for adventurous eaters. This plant-based-friendly version is a healthier twist on traditional bibimbap.
instructions: Heat a char-grill or grill pan over medium-high heat.
Grill sliced Romano peppers until slightly charred, about 3-4 minutes per side.
In a separate pan, heat 1 tablespoon of toasted sesame oil over medium heat.
Add sliced zucchini and cook until tender, about 3-4 minutes.
Add julienne carrots and cook for an additional 2 minutes.
In a large bowl, combine cooked brown rice, grilled Romano peppers, zucchini, and carrots.
Drizzle with soy sauce and gochujang sauce, and toss to combine.
Sprinkle toasted pine nuts, sesame seeds, and pickled ginger on top.
Garnish with thinly sliced scallions and serve immediately.
Note: this recipe is for refrigerator pickling/preserving only — store in the refrigerator and consume within 1 week. Do not water-bath can without an acid (vinegar or lemon juice).
